guidelines_resources.title: Head and Neck Cancer Survivorship Care Guideline (2016)
guidelines_resources.notes: Components of survivorship care including surveillance, late effects, and health promotion [Cohen 2016].

guidelines_resources.title: Prevention and Management of Osteoradionecrosis in Patients with Head and Neck Cancer Treated with Radiation Therapy (2024)
guidelines_resources.notes: Evidence-based recommendations for ORN risk assessment, prevention, and management [Peterson 2024].

key_trials.full_name: Pembrolizumab Alone or with Chemotherapy versus Cetuximab with Chemotherapy for Recurrent or Metastatic Squamous Cell Carcinoma of the Head and Neck
key_trials.population: Recurrent or metastatic HNSCC, no prior systemic therapy for recurrent/metastatic disease (except if given as part of multimodal treatment completed >6 months prior)
key_trials.primary_endpoint: Overall survival in CPS ≥1 population (pembrolizumab alone) and total population (pembrolizumab + chemotherapy)
key_trials.key_result: Pembrolizumab + chemo improved OS vs EXTREME in total population (median 13.0 vs 10.7 months; HR 0.77; 95% CI 0.63–0.93; P=0.003). Pembrolizumab alone improved OS vs EXTREME in CPS ≥20 (median 14.9 vs 10.7 months; HR 0.61; 95% CI 0.45–0.83) and CPS ≥1 (median 12.3 vs 10.3 months; HR 0.78; 95% CI 0.64–0.96).
key_trials.secondary_outcomes: PFS not different between pembrolizumab + chemo and EXTREME; longer duration of response with pembrolizumab. Long-term follow-up (Harrington 2023) confirmed OS benefit.
key_trials.practice_change: Established pembrolizumab alone (for CPS ≥1) and pembrolizumab + platinum/5-FU (any CPS) as preferred first-line therapy for recurrent/metastatic HNSCC.

key_trials.full_name: Neoadjuvant and Adjuvant Pembrolizumab in Locally Advanced Head and Neck Cancer
key_trials.population: Resectable locally advanced HNSCC (stage III–IVA), PD-L1 CPS ≥1; >95% HPV-negative
key_trials.intervention: Neoadjuvant pembrolizumab → surgery → adjuvant pembrolizumab/RT (with cisplatin if ENE/positive margin) → adjuvant pembrolizumab
key_trials.comparator: Placebo → surgery → adjuvant RT ± cisplatin (standard of care)
key_trials.key_result: 36-month EFS: 59.8% (pembrolizumab) vs 45.9% (control) in CPS ≥10 (HR 0.66; 95% CI 0.49–0.88; P=0.004); 58.2% vs 44.9% in CPS ≥1 (HR 0.70; 95% CI 0.55–0.89; P=0.003). OS not yet significant.
key_trials.secondary_outcomes: Pathological complete response 3.0% in total population; major pathological response 9.3%. Reduced distant metastases rate. Higher TRAEs grade ≥3: 44.6% vs 42.9%.
key_trials.practice_change: Led to FDA approval of neoadjuvant/adjuvant pembrolizumab for resectable, PD-L1 positive (CPS ≥1) locally advanced SCCHN. Now included in NCCN Guidelines as an option for eligible oral cavity, p16-negative oropharynx, hypopharynx, and larynx cancers.

key_trials.full_name: Nivolumab for Recurrent Squamous-Cell Carcinoma of the Head and Neck
key_trials.population: Recurrent SCCHN with progression within 6 months of platinum-based chemotherapy
key_trials.comparator: Standard single-agent therapy (methotrexate, docetaxel, or cetuximab)
key_trials.key_result: Median OS 7.5 months (nivolumab) vs 5.1 months (standard); HR 0.70; 97.73% CI 0.51–0.96; P=0.01. 1-year OS 36.0% vs 16.6%.
key_trials.secondary_outcomes: ORR 13.3% vs 5.8%; median PFS not significantly different (2.0 vs 2.3 months).
key_trials.practice_change: Established nivolumab as standard second-line therapy for platinum-refractory recurrent/metastatic HNSCC (category 1). Two FDA-approved dosing regimens: 240 mg every 2 weeks or 480 mg every 4 weeks.

key_trials.full_name: Long-Term Results of RTOG 91-11: A Comparison of Three Nonsurgical Treatment Strategies to Preserve the Larynx in Patients with Locally Advanced Larynx Cancer
key_trials.population: Stage III–IV laryngeal cancer (excluding T1 and high-volume T4)
key_trials.intervention: Arm 1: Induction cisplatin/5-FU → RT; Arm 2: Concurrent cisplatin/RT; Arm 3: RT alone
key_trials.primary_endpoint: Larynx preservation, overall survival, locoregional control
key_trials.key_result: 2-year larynx preservation: 88% (concurrent), 74% (induction), 69% (RT alone) – significantly better for concurrent. No significant OS difference between arms at long-term follow-up (10-year OS not different). More non-cancer deaths in concurrent arm.
key_trials.secondary_outcomes: Locoregional control better with concurrent. Late toxicity similar.
key_trials.practice_change: Concurrent cisplatin/RT became the preferred larynx preservation strategy for advanced laryngeal cancer requiring total laryngectomy.

key_trials.full_name: Toripalimab Plus Chemotherapy for Recurrent or Metastatic Nasopharyngeal Carcinoma: The JUPITER-02 Randomized Clinical Trial
key_trials.journal: Journal of the American Medical Association
key_trials.population: Recurrent or metastatic nasopharyngeal carcinoma, no prior systemic therapy for recurrent/metastatic disease
key_trials.key_result: Median PFS 21.4 months (toripalimab) vs 8.2 months (placebo); HR 0.52; 95% CI 0.37–0.73. OS HR 0.63 (not yet reached vs 33.7 months). ORR 78.8% vs 64.6%.
key_trials.secondary_outcomes: Duration of response longer; manageable toxicity.
key_trials.practice_change: Toripalimab + GC became preferred first-line therapy for recurrent/metastatic NPC (category 1). Toripalimab monotherapy also preferred for subsequent-line (category 1) [Wang 2021 POLARIS-02].

key_trials.full_name: Postoperative Concurrent Radiotherapy and Chemotherapy for High-Risk Squamous-Cell Carcinoma of the Head and Neck (and parallel EORTC trial)
key_trials.population: High-risk resected SCCHN (RTOG: ≥2 positive nodes, positive margins, or ENE; EORTC: also perineural/vascular invasion, level IV/V nodes from oral cavity/oropharynx)
key_trials.intervention: Postoperative cisplatin (100 mg/m2 q3wk × 3) + RT (60–66 Gy)
key_trials.key_result: EORTC: improved OS (P=0.02) and progression-free survival. RTOG: improved locoregional control and DFS, but not OS. Combined analysis showed benefit only in patients with ENE and/or positive margins [Bernier 2005].
key_trials.practice_change: Established cisplatin + RT as standard postoperative therapy for high-risk features (ENE and/or positive margins, category 1). Postoperative RT alone for intermediate-risk features.

key_trials.full_name: Platinum-Based Chemotherapy plus Cetuximab in Head and Neck Cancer
key_trials.population: Recurrent or metastatic SCCHN, no prior systemic therapy for recurrent/metastatic disease
key_trials.practice_change: Established the EXTREME regimen as first-line palliative therapy for recurrent/metastatic SCCHN. Now largely replaced by pembrolizumab-based regimens as preferred first-line.
